name: Turkey-Chili Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 small fresh turkey (discard giblets or save for another use)
2 medium potatoes, cooked, peeled and mashed slightly
3 tablespoons chipotle puree (soak chilies in water, then puree)
recipeInstructions:
To prepare stock: Place all ingredients in a stockpot; fill with cold water until the turkey is just covered. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, for 1 1/2 hours or until the turkey is cooked. Remove the turkey. When cool enough to handle, remove the meat from the bones. Return the bones to the stockpot. Refrigerate the meat. Discard the skin. Cover the stock and simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours. Strain; discard solids. Reserve 6 cups of stock and 2 cups finely diced turkey for soup.
To prepare soup:
Heat oil in a soup pot. Add onion and garlic; saute until onions are translucent. Add celery, carrot, and potatoes. Cover and cook slowly over medium heat for 30 minutes, or until the vegetables are soft but not browned, stirring occasionally. Add chili powder, chipotle puree, cumin, paprika, and cinnamon. Cook over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add tomatoes, reserved stock, beans, bell peppers, wine, and cilantro; simmer for 35 to 40 minutes. Add the reserved diced turkey, mustard greens, corn, and vinegar. Simmer for an additional 15 minutes. Season to taste with salt, pepper, Tabasco sauce (optional), and lemon juice (optional). Serve with 1 or 2 garnishes.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
Per serving: 524 Calories; 10g Fat (16% calories from fat); 27g Protein; 88g Carbohydrate; 0mg Cholesterol; 201mg Sodium
